summary: Strong, well-targeted SRE resume that stays within the candidate's real facts; only minor consistency/tense polish and a couple of medium JD-keyword gaps remain.

issues.issue: Verb-tense inconsistency: the brief mandates ALL experience bullets use past tense (no current employer), but the CRM project leads with present/past mixes that are fine, while the first project bullet 'Set up and maintained' is past — acceptable. However the summary uses present participles ('sustaining', 'driving', 'authoring') which can read as ongoing/current; the candidate is unemployed and the role ended Apr 2026.
issues.where: Summary: "sustaining **99.9% uptime SLA** ... driving incident response ... authoring runbooks and blameless post-mortems"
issues.suggestion: Reframe to past-aligned phrasing (e.g., 'who sustained 99.9% uptime ... drove incident response ... authored runbooks') to honor the past-tense rule for the now-ended role.

issues.issue: Role title hybrid 'Site Reliability Engineer (DevOps Engineer)' is a tailored relabel. The candidate's actual title was 'DevOps Engineer'. The parenthetical keeps it honest, but mixing the JD title into the role line risks appearing as a title change rather than the real title.
issues.suggestion: Consider 'DevOps Engineer (SRE focus)' or simply 'DevOps Engineer' to put the real title first, since profile lists DevOps Engineer as the actual title.

issues.issue: Skills list 'OpenShift', 'Datadog', 'ELK Stack', 'Ansible', 'AWS X-Ray' which are Tier-2/exposure-only per the brief. These are allowed (he can claim them), but OpenShift/Datadog/Istio are explicitly 'include only if JD asks' — verify the Calix JD references them; otherwise they slightly dilute focus.
issues.suggestion: Keep only if the JD mentions them; otherwise trim Tier-2 items to keep the SRE focus tight (these are defensible but not deep).

issues.issue: Common SRE JD keywords are not surfaced. Without the full JD text I flag likely high-value misses: 'MTTR', 'on-call', 'alerting/PagerDuty', 'error budget', 'capacity planning', 'automation/tooling', 'SLI'. The resume has SLO/SLA and 'on-call response readiness' but not MTTR or alerting tooling.
issues.where: Summary and bullets reference 'SLO monitoring' but no 'MTTR', 'alerting', or 'SLI'.
issues.suggestion: Where defensible (brief allows PagerDuty alerts routing and MTTR-style framing in B10), add 'reduced MTTR' / 'alerting' / 'SLI' phrasing to hit ATS keywords.

issues.issue: Location mismatch surfacing: JD is Bangalore; resume location is Ahmedabad with no relocation/remote note. ATS/recruiter may filter on location.
issues.suggestion: Profile lists Bangalore among onsite-available cities — consider adding 'Open to Bangalore (onsite/hybrid)' near contact line if format permits.

issues.issue: Bullet repetition across Experience and Projects: the EKS upgrade (v1.33 → v1.34) and Prometheus/Grafana/Loki/Alloy observability appear in both the experience section and the CRM project, creating near-duplicate content.
issues.where: experience[0] bullet 4 and projects[0] bullet 1 both describe 'EKS upgrade (v1.33 → v1.34)' and 'Prometheus, Grafana, Loki, and Grafana Alloy'.
issues.suggestion: Differentiate: keep the upgrade in experience and let the project bullet focus on DR/cost, or vice versa, to avoid redundancy on a one-page resume.

issues.issue: Education line uses double spaces around the pipe separator, inconsistent with single-space separators elsewhere.
issues.suggestion: Normalize to single spaces around the pipe: 'India | CGPA: 7.25 / 10'.

issues.issue: Healthcare prototype bullets correctly drop the flagged production-scale metrics ('500+ users', '5+ IAM roles', '30% latency'). Confirmed clean — no fabrication. Noting as verified, not an issue.
issues.suggestion: No change needed — good handling of prototype-scale claims.
